title: albizia elata
intro: Albizia elata, also known as the East Indian walnut or Bengal walnut, is a tree species native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The bark and seeds of the tree have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, offering benefits like reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and relief from gastrointestinal discomfort. Herbal preparations of Albizia elata include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and capsules for various therapeutic applications.
